summary: June 5 slate: 5 directional wins and 8 losses across 13 graded matchups (38.5%), with 2 NO_PICK (PENDING_SP_DATA). The only staked bet — BAL @ TOR GOLD, placed via the near-zero CAP-1 rescue rule — lost 3–13 in a blowout, marking a 0W-1L day on actual action. Key process failure: the rescue fired despite confidence_downgrade=True being set on BAL@TOR. Slate-wide, SKIP decisions correctly avoided 7 directional losses including SF@CHC (18-3) and WSH@ARI (14-1) blowouts. Critical learnings: confidence_downgrade=True must permanently block the near-zero edge CAP-1 rescue, and all 5 games with f5_full_delta > 0.17 resolved with Stage 2 as the correct predictor.

SF @ CHC.headline: SF exploded 18-3 in a historic blowout; HARD CAP 4 (Stage 1/2 delta=0.24) and carry-failure pattern correctly blocked stake.
SF @ CHC.hypothesis: Process win despite massive directional loss. HARD CAP 4 (f5_full_delta=0.2386, confidence_downgrade=True) combined with the near-zero bullpen gap (|hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap|=0.0085) blocked stake on multiple independent grounds. Stage 2 (full_prob=0.5708) correctly predicted CHC vulnerability while Stage 1 (f5_prob=0.8094) was wildly overconfident — the 18-3 margin validates Stage 2's full-game carry concern exactly. No process failure; the SKIP criteria fired correctly and protected against a catastrophic loss.
SF @ CHC.signals_to_recheck: f5_full_delta (0.2386) + hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap (0.0085, near-zero) + [HARD CAP 4] Stage_1_2_disagree_with_confidence_downgrade — carry-thesis failure pattern confirmed at extreme scale (18-3 blowout).

SEA @ DET.headline: DET won 7-3; D-grade SKIP maintained; dual opposing signals (SP xERA, PQI) were wrong directionally — consistent with D-grade coin-flip.
SEA @ DET.hypothesis: Directional win on correctly SKIP-tiered D-grade pick. SP edge 1.50 xERA AGAINST DET (Woo over Valdez per model) and PQI=-28.3 both opposed the pick; Valdez delivered and DET won 7-3 anyway. Near-zero bullpen gap (|gap|=0.0001) and f5_full_delta=0.1873 also correctly blocked stake. Consistent with the 6/3 ruling: D-grade dual-signal-against is unreliable, not anti-directional. No process learning.
SEA @ DET.signals_to_recheck: pen_strain_pick_side (0.1047, high collision risk) + hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap (0.0001, near-zero) — DET wins 7-3 despite dual opposing signals; D-grade directional variance confirmed.

CHW @ PHI.headline: PHI won 8-6; SP edge 2.52 xERA (Luzardo over Kay) was decisive; D-grade SKIP correctly avoided directional loss.
CHW @ PHI.hypothesis: Process win; directional loss on correctly SKIP-tiered D-grade. SP edge 2.52 xERA AGAINST CHW (Luzardo over Kay) materialized — PHI won 8-6. PQI=+32.3 against CHW and the largest f5_full_delta on the slate (0.3023) with near-zero bullpen gap (|gap|=0.0057) blocked stake on multiple grounds. The calibration_caution_18_25pp flag (edge=+19.86pp) further validated the SKIP. When SP xERA gap exceeds 2.0 against the pick on D-grade games, the opposing direction is near-reliable (3/3 validated today: CHW@PHI, CIN@STL, WSH@ARI).
CHW @ PHI.signals_to_recheck: f5_full_delta (0.3023) + calibration_caution_18_25pp (edge=+19.86pp) + [HARD CAP 4] Stage_1_2_disagree_with_confidence_downgrade — PHI wins 8-6; SP xERA gap > 2.0 against D-grade pick reliably predicts opposing direction.

BOS @ NYY.headline: BOS won 5-3; HARD CAP 4 (delta=0.20) and negative edge correctly blocked stake; Weathers SP xERA advantage failed to hold.
BOS @ NYY.hypothesis: Process win. HARD CAP 4 (f5_full_delta=0.2034, confidence_downgrade=True) combined with negative edge (-1.03pp) and near-zero bullpen gap (|gap|=0.0104) correctly blocked the PLATINUM-labeled pick. Ryan Weathers (NYY home) held a slight xERA edge but NYY lost 3-5 — Stage 2 full_prob=0.569 was directionally correct while Stage 1 (f5_prob=0.7724) was overconfident. This is the third game today where SP xERA edge agreeing with the model pick produced a LOSS under Stage 1/2 disagreement conditions.
BOS @ NYY.signals_to_recheck: f5_full_delta (0.2034) + [HARD CAP 4] Stage_1_2_disagree_with_confidence_downgrade + [HARD CAP 1] negative_edge_GOLD (-1.03pp) — NYY loses 3-5; SP xERA edge 'agreeing with pick' was 0W-3L today against Stage 1/2 disagreement.

BAL @ TOR.headline: BAL won 13-3 in a blowout; the near-zero CAP-1 rescue to GOLD was a process failure — confidence_downgrade=True should have blocked rescue.
BAL @ TOR.hypothesis: PROCESS FAILURE. Claude applied the 6/1 CAP-1 near-zero rescue rule (|edge_pp|=0.04pp, pre-cap A-, 3 confirming signals) despite confidence_downgrade=True being set. The confidence_downgrade flag signals structural model uncertainty — a near-zero market edge cannot resolve it; it only means the market also lacks conviction. Trey Yesavage (TOR, home) and Brandon Young (BAL, away) are both developing pitchers; the SP xERA advantage was built on thin-sample data. BAL's lineup overwhelmed TOR 13-3. Rule update required: the near-zero CAP-1 rescue window is suspended when confidence_downgrade=True — both CAP-1 and CAP-10 demotions stand permanently when this flag is True.
BAL @ TOR.signals_to_recheck: confidence_downgrade_True + [HARD CAP 1] negative_edge_GOLD (0.04pp, near-zero rounding artifact) — BAL wins 13-3 blowout; CAP-1 rescue with confidence_downgrade=True is the process failure; rule: rescue requires confidence_downgrade=False.

TB @ MIA.headline: TB won 6-0; PENDING_SP_DATA protocol (Ryan Gusto 52 pitches) correctly blocked directional commitment on a decisive away win.
TB @ MIA.hypothesis: Protocol win. Ryan Gusto's 52 Statcast pitches correctly triggered the hard stop per the 100-pitch minimum threshold. TB won 6-0 — a result that would have represented a large LOSS had MIA been picked. The PENDING protocol's protective value is again evident. No directional learning as model uncertainty was genuine.
TB @ MIA.signals_to_recheck: thin_sp_data (Ryan Gusto, 52 Statcast pitches below 100 threshold) — TB wins 6-0; PENDING_SP_DATA protocol validated; high-variance thin-SP outcomes confirmed.

PIT @ ATL.headline: ATL won 6-3; HARD CAP 1 at -4.31pp correctly blocked stake on a directional win; BvP triple-OWNER cluster predicted direction but not EV.
PIT @ ATL.hypothesis: Process win; directional win noted but no stake missed in error. ATL's triple-OWNER BvP cluster vs Keller (Acuña shrunk 0.988, Olson shrunk 0.917, Dubón shrunk 0.783) correctly predicted ATL winning 6-3. HARD CAP 1 at -4.31pp — just outside the |edge_pp| < 4pp rescue window — was correctly enforced per the 6/4 ruling. BvP OWNERs predict direction but cannot create positive EV when the market has already priced ATL beyond model fair value. The directional read was correct; the market pricing was also correct.
PIT @ ATL.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 1] negative_edge_GOLD (-4.31pp) + F3_swing_take_gap (906.8) — ATL wins 6-3; BvP triple-OWNER cluster is directionally reliable but cannot override genuine negative market EV; HARD CAP 1 at -4.31pp enforcement was correct.

OAK @ HOU.headline: HOU won 5-1; Claude DOWNGRADE from GOLD D-grade was correct process; directional miss on a game where Stage 1/2 agreed.
OAK @ HOU.hypothesis: Process-sound DOWNGRADE that missed a directional WIN. GOLD-labeled D-grade (-1) correctly triggered DOWNGRADE: SP edge 0.70 xERA AGAINST HOU (Lambert over Perkins), PQI=-11.4, F3=380.6 (below 1000 threshold), and bullpen_fatigue all opposed the GOLD label. HOU won 5-1, validating the directional pick. Stage 1/2 agreed (delta=0.0864), suggesting the directional read was genuinely correct even against quantitative opposing signals. Classified as process-sound miss, not a process failure — the DOWNGRADE was justified given the D-grade contradiction.
OAK @ HOU.signals_to_recheck: F3_swing_take_gap (380.6, below 1000 high-conviction threshold) + hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap (+0.0019, near-zero) + pen_strain_pick_side (0.1046) — HOU wins 5-1 despite opposing signals; GOLD-label D-grade contradiction DOWNGRADE was correct process.

CIN @ STL.headline: STL won 10-3; Stage 2 full_prob correctly identified STL over Stage 1 CIN; f5_full_delta=0.178 blocked stake on losing direction.
CIN @ STL.hypothesis: Process win. The CSV pick column (CIN, f5-derived Stage 1) was wrong while Stage 2 (full_prob=0.3492 favoring STL) was correct — STL won 10-3. f5_full_delta=0.178 with confidence_downgrade=True, acute_roster, and bullpen_fatigue stress flags combined to correctly SKIP. The calibration_caution_18_25pp stress (edge=+23.02pp inflated) was also validated. This is the 5th game today where f5_full_delta > 0.17 resolved with Stage 2 as the correct predictor — the pattern is now 5/5 on this slate.
CIN @ STL.signals_to_recheck: f5_full_delta (0.178) + calibration_caution_18_25pp (edge=+23.02pp) + acute_roster_True + confidence_downgrade_True — STL wins 10-3; f5_full_delta > 0.17 → Stage 2 correct predictor (5/5 today).

CLE @ TEX.headline: TEX won 3-2 narrowly; D-grade SKIP maintained; SP xERA and PQI both against TEX were directionally wrong.
CLE @ TEX.hypothesis: Directional win on correctly SKIP-tiered D-grade. SP edge 1.40 xERA AGAINST TEX (Rocker over Messick) and PQI=-19.6 were both wrong directionally — TEX won 3-2. Stage 1/2 agreement (delta=0.04) was the only positive signal. hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=-0.0158 (TEX bullpen slightly better) may have been a hidden tailwind. Narrow 3-2 margin is consistent with genuine uncertainty; this is D-grade directional coin-flip variance, not a process learning event.
CLE @ TEX.signals_to_recheck: hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap (-0.0158, negative = TEX pen better) + SP xERA (Rocker 1.40 over Messick, AGAINST pick, directionally wrong) — TEX wins 3-2; D-grade opposing signals are unreliable directionally.

KC @ MIN.headline: MIN won 5-3; HARD CAP 1 (genuine -1.42pp) and CAP 10 correctly blocked stake on directional win; small-sample Wacha xERA held.
KC @ MIN.hypothesis: Process win; directional win correctly not staked. HARD CAP 1 at -1.42pp was a genuine negative edge — distinct from BAL@TOR's near-rounding-artifact 0.04pp. Pre-cap B+ with small-sample SP edge (Wacha, min_pa=95), PQI=+14.1, and Stage 1/2 agreement confirmed MIN direction, and MIN won 5-3. Negative EV at model odds is non-rescuable regardless of directional signal quality. CAP-1 and CAP-10 demotions both stood correctly. The contrast with BAL@TOR is instructive: genuine negative edges (-1.42pp) and near-rounding-artifact edges (0.04pp) are categorically different.
KC @ MIN.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 1] negative_edge_GOLD (-1.42pp, genuine) + small_sample_SP_True (Wacha, min_pa=95) + confidence_downgrade_True — MIN wins 5-3; genuine negative edge is non-rescuable regardless of directional signal quality.

MIL @ COL.headline: MIL won 9-7 at Coors; largest f5_full_delta on the slate (0.3144) correctly blocked stake; SP xERA against COL was directionally right.
MIL @ COL.hypothesis: Process win; directional loss on correctly SKIP-tiered D-grade. SP edge 1.22 xERA AGAINST COL (Sproat over Feltner), PQI=-16.8, Stage 1/2 disagree (Δ=0.3144 — largest on slate), fair_prob=0.3965 (below 0.42 threshold), and near-zero bullpen gap (|gap|=0.0014) all opposed the pick. MIL won 9-7 in a high-scoring Coors game, validating all directional signals. Stage 2 was again correct on a game with f5_full_delta > 0.17. No stake, no loss.
MIL @ COL.signals_to_recheck: f5_full_delta (0.3144, largest on slate) + hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap (+0.0014, near-zero) — MIL wins 9-7; Stage 2 again correct predictor when f5_full_delta > 0.17; Coors Field home-SP uncertainty (Feltner) noted.

WSH @ ARI.headline: WSH won 14-1 in the slate's largest blowout; SP xERA gap of 2.86 (Kelly over Griffin) against ARI was decisively validated.
WSH @ ARI.hypothesis: Process win; directional loss on correctly SKIP-tiered D-grade. The largest blowout of the slate — 14-1. SP edge 2.86 xERA AGAINST ARI (Merrill Kelly over Foster Griffin) was dramatically validated — Griffin was overwhelmed and WSH scored 14. PQI=-21.1, f5_full_delta=0.2529, and sub-meaningful bullpen gap (|gap|=0.0215) blocked stake on multiple grounds. Today's three D-grade games with SP xERA gap > 2.0 against the pick all resolved correctly in the opposing direction (CHW@PHI 8-6, CIN@STL 10-3, WSH@ARI 14-1). No stake, no loss.
WSH @ ARI.signals_to_recheck: SP xERA gap (Kelly 2.86 over Griffin, AGAINST pick) + f5_full_delta (0.2529) + hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap (+0.0215, sub-meaningful) — WSH wins 14-1; SP xERA gaps > 2.0 against D-grade pick were 3/3 correct in opposing direction today.

NYM @ SD.headline: NYM won 5-0 in a shutout; single-signal pre-cap B- with negative edge correctly maintained SKIP; Michael King dominated.
NYM @ SD.hypothesis: Process win; directional loss on correctly SKIP-tiered pick. SD held pick_prob=0.5194 (barely above 50%), pre-cap score=1 with only Stage 1/2 agreement as a positive signal — the weakest non-PENDING signal stack on the slate. Edge=-3.17pp was negative; no F-signal, SP xERA confirming SD, or PQI were present. Michael King (NYM away) dominated Christian Scott (SD home) for a 5-0 shutout. The single-signal pre-cap B- with negative edge was correctly SKIP-tiered. No variance — the model's marginal confidence was appropriate.
NYM @ SD.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 1] negative_edge_GOLD (-3.17pp) + single Stage 1/2 agreement signal only — NYM wins 5-0; single-signal pre-cap B- with negative edge is insufficient for any rescue consideration.

LAA @ LAD.headline: LAD won 1-0 in a tight game; PENDING_SP_DATA (LAA SP TBD) correctly blocked directional commitment.
LAA @ LAD.hypothesis: Protocol win. LAA's probable SP was not yet announced, triggering the hard stop. LAD won 1-0 in an extremely tight game — any directional pick would have carried unknown edge. The PENDING protocol prevents exactly this uncertainty from driving stakes. No directional learning.
LAA @ LAD.signals_to_recheck: thin_sp_data (LAA SP not announced) — LAD wins 1-0; PENDING_SP_DATA protocol validated; tight margins in thin-SP games confirm genuine unpredictability.

patterns_observed: CRITICAL — confidence_downgrade=True permanently suspends the near-zero CAP-1 rescue rule: BAL@TOR (loss 13-3) was upgraded to GOLD via the 6/1 rescue window (|edge_pp|=0.04pp, pre-cap A-, 3 confirming signals) despite confidence_downgrade=True being set. The flag signals structural model uncertainty that a near-zero market edge cannot resolve — near-zero market edge means the market also lacks conviction, not that the model is correct. Rule update: the CAP-1 rescue window (|edge_pp| < 4pp + pre-cap A- + 3 independent signals) is suspended when confidence_downgrade=True; both CAP-1 and CAP-10 demotions stand permanently in this case regardless of edge proximity to zero.

f5_full_delta > 0.17 resolved with Stage 2 as the correct predictor in all 5 applicable games today (SF@CHC Δ=0.24, BOS@NYY Δ=0.20, CIN@STL Δ=0.18, MIL@COL Δ=0.31, WSH@ARI Δ=0.25) — in every case the full_prob side (Stage 2) identified the winner while the pick column (Stage 1 f5-derived) was wrong. Reinforce as a hard disqualifier: when f5_full_delta >= 0.17, Stage 2 is authoritative regardless of pre-cap grade or BvP signals; SKIP is mandatory and no rescue path exists.

SP xERA 'agrees with pick' signals produced 0W-3L today (SF@CHC, BOS@NYY, BAL@TOR); all three involved at least one developing-pitcher starter with thin Statcast sample (Cabrera, Weathers, Yesavage). SP xERA-based confirmation is unreliable when either starter is a developing pitcher — treat confidence_downgrade=True or F1* (small_sample_SP_True) as a signal that corroborating F2/F3/PQI are required before accepting SP xERA as a rescue-worthy confirmer for any cap override.
